q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is target.write_bytes(body), a filesystem write operation that is an irreversible side effect and potentially dangerous via path traversal.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated; decision made at Q1.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision made at Q1.

mock_guidance.target: Mock pathlib.Path.write_bytes so that the filesystem write is intercepted.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the target path (str(target)) and the body bytes passed to write_bytes. These are the key values needed to assert path traversal and verify the intended write location.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Make write_bytes a no-op that records its arguments instead of performing the actual write. Allow the function to continue and return its normal dictionary.
notes: The function returns {'stored': str(target), 'bytes': len(body)}, which can be used alongside the captured mock arguments to verify both the path and payload.
Ensure the mock is scoped so other Path operations (e.g., the / join on line 121) are not affected.
